message:
feat(storage): tick 142 token-lifecycle clusters cx-first siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Twenty-first sibling-expansion tick. Adds 4 new storage `_with_cx`
siblings across two related token-lifecycle clusters.

New siblings:
- get_approval_token_by_code_with_cx (write-channel routed read —
  legacy path uses the writer queue to serialise access)
- consume_approval_token_by_code_with_cx (write path)
- insert_prepared_plan_with_cx (write path)
- consume_prepared_plan_with_cx (write path)

Each sibling does a pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()?` for cancellation
budget honouring, then delegates to the unchanged legacy impl.

Test: `storage_tick142_token_lifecycle_clusters_roundtrip` exercises
both clusters on a single fresh DB — seeds a pane for FK constraints,
inserts an approval token with far-future expires_at (consume_sync
enforces `expires_at >= now_ms()`), confirms get-then-consume-then-
second-consume semantics (second consume returns None; token is
single-use), then same flow for prepared plans. Verified via fork
bypass: 489/489 storage:: tests pass.

Storage cx-first coverage: 97 + 4 = 101 siblings across 140 legacy
pub async fns. 39 legacy methods still lack a sibling; approval-
token-code and prepared-plan clusters now fully covered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
